name: National Faith Homebuyers, Atlanta Branch
description_short: Non-profit homebuyer education and counseling organization offering free first-time homebuyer assistance, down payment help, and foreclosure prevention across Michigan and Georgia.
description_long:
National Faith HomeBuyers was founded in 1996 by Dina Harris in Washtenaw County, Michigan, originally as the Washtenaw Homebuyers Program before expanding to Detroit and eventually Atlanta. The organization is built on the principle that homeownership should be accessible to everyone, regardless of background or financial situation. With over 25 years of operation, the organization has counseled more than 7,000 families and facilitated over 1,000 first-time home purchases. The founder brings 40+ years of housing industry experience to the organization's mission.

National Faith HomeBuyers offers a comprehensive suite of free services designed to guide clients through every stage of homeownership. Their core offerings include homebuyer education classes, down payment assistance programs (up to $25,000 in some jurisdictions), foreclosure prevention and assistance workshops, financial literacy training, credit education, and HUD-approved housing counseling. All services are provided at no cost to clients. The organization hosts virtual workshops and in-person information sessions monthly, with clients required to attend an initial session before scheduling one-on-one housing counseling appointments. They maintain an active events calendar and encourage registration through their Eventbrite page.

What distinguishes National Faith HomeBuyers is their explicitly non-judgmental, compassionate approach combined with deep expertise in the housing industry. The organization specifically targets first-time homebuyers and those at risk of foreclosure, populations that often find the homebuying process overwhelming. They emphasize financial responsibility and client empowerment rather than simply processing applications. The founder's personal experience as a first-time homebuyer who needed assistance directly informs the organization's client-centered philosophy. Their expansion to multiple states demonstrates growing recognition of their model's effectiveness.

Honest assessment: National Faith HomeBuyers is a legitimate, experienced non-profit that delivers genuine value through free education and counseling. However, availability is limited to Detroit, Westland, and Atlanta locations—geographic constraints that exclude most of the country. Down payment assistance appears contingent on local municipal programs (referenced through Detroit's partnership), meaning availability and award amounts vary by location. The organization focuses on education and counseling rather than direct lending, so clients still need to secure mortgages through traditional lenders after completing their programs.
pros: All services are completely free—no hidden fees or charges
HUD-approved housing counselor services with specialized foreclosure prevention assistance
Down payment assistance programs providing up to $25,000 (where available through municipal partnerships)
Founder and staff have 40+ years combined housing industry expertise
Documented track record: 7,000+ families counseled, 1,000+ first-time purchases facilitated since 1996
Non-judgmental, compassionate approach specifically designed for first-time and struggling homebuyers
Multiple service options including virtual workshops, group classes, and one-on-one counseling
cons: Geographic limitation: only serves Detroit, Westland (Michigan), and Atlanta—unavailable for most U.S. consumers
Down payment assistance contingent on local government programs, so availability and amounts vary significantly by location
Requires attendance at initial information session before accessing housing counselor appointments, creating time commitment barrier
Organization provides education and counseling but cannot directly originate mortgages, so clients must still qualify through traditional lenders
Website lacks specific details on income limits, eligibility requirements, or typical down payment assistance award amounts
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Detroit, Westland, or Atlanta with limited down payment savings
Homeowners facing foreclosure or at risk of foreclosure in served markets
Low-to-moderate income families needing financial literacy and credit education before applying for mortgages
Individuals intimidated by the homebuying process who benefit from hands-on counseling and step-by-step guidance
diagnosis: National Faith HomeBuyers is ideal for first-time homebuyers and at-risk homeowners in the Detroit, Westland, or Atlanta areas who need free education, counseling, and potential down payment assistance. The main caveat is strict geographic availability—the organization only operates in three metro areas—and reliance on local municipal programs for down payment assistance, meaning award amounts and eligibility vary by location and funding availability.
services: Down payment assistance programs (up to $25,000 where available)
Information sessions and Fast Track workshops (monthly)
Home buyer process guidance and navigation
Resources including applications, checklists, and educational materials
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2470 Windy Hill Rd Se, Suite 315, Marietta, GA 30067
